items.title: ‘There was a catch’: My doctor friend was outraged by his hospital’s fundraising tactics. Where do we draw the line?
items.description: “This shocking program was exploiting doctors and the trust inherent in their relationships with patients.”

items.title: Warsh’s throwaway comment injects life into the debasement trade, for one day at least
items.description: As much as new Fed Chair Kevin Warsh tried to refrain from steering markets, it looks like he did.

items.title: Tamera Mowry lists Bay Area house for $2.7 million as she prepares to build Napa Valley ‘dream home’
items.description: “Sister, Sister” star Tamera Mowry-Housley has listed her longtime California house for $2.7 million—months after revealing that she and her husband are in the process of building a new home in Napa V

items.title: After eight years, Google loses final appeal against $4.7 billion European Union fine
items.description: The Court of Justice of the European Union ruled to uphold its fine against Google over alleged anti-competition practices

items.title: OpenAI reportedly considers handing 5% stake to the government. Here’s why the AI lab would consider that move.
items.description: Bringing the government on board as an equity partner may serve to dilute some of the criticism of the AI industry that has developed momentum in recent months.

items.title: Protein-packed Cheerios and cat food: How General Mills plans to combat a tough spending backdrop
items.description: “Cat growth is on fire,” one executive said.

items.title: Opening a ‘Trump account’ for your children? Here is the risk you need to reckon with first.
items.description: The new accounts ban bonds and international stocks, forcing parents to bet their children’s financial future entirely on U.S. equities.

items.title: Nvidia is betting on a trillion-dollar robotics boom. Here is the hidden way to trade it.
items.description: CEO Jensen Huang has called humanoid robots a “multitrillion-dollar economic opportunity.”

items.title: ‘We were stunned’: My daughter, 39, said her mother-in-law gives her more money than we do. Do I call her out?
items.description: “She thanked us for the $100 birthday gift and then announced that her mother-in-law had sent her $400.”

items.title: CoreWeave, Nebius shares tumble as Meta stands to become a fresh threat in the cloud
items.description: Meta’s reported interest in monetizing its AI infrastructure is leading investors to question the sustainability of neocloud business models.
